records.function: Launch the packaged Wisp app.
records.production_entry_point: fresh PyInstaller artifact -> Wisp executable -> frozen supervisor dispatch -> real UI/native/brain/audio workers
success_assertions: A freshly built packaged executable shows the real overlay, receives successful pings from four distinct worker processes, starts flows/hotkeys, exits cleanly, and leaves no managed process behind.
records.audit_note: The release build for Windows, Linux, and macOS now runs the same process-level smoke before packaging; the local Windows artifact was rebuilt and executed successfully.

records.function: Launch Wisp from the source checkout and development launchers.
records.production_entry_point: platform Start Wisp development launcher -> runtime.supervisor.app -> real UI/native/brain/audio workers
success_assertions: The platform source launcher uses a compatible provisioned Python, shows the real overlay, receives successful pings from four distinct worker processes, starts flows/hotkeys, exits cleanly, and leaves no managed process behind.
records.audit_note: Writable data, user state, add-ons, and the single-instance lock are isolated without replacing the production launcher or supervisor path.

records.function: Prevent a second Wisp instance from running at the same time.
records.production_entry_point: runtime.supervisor.app.main -> core.system.single_instance.acquire
success_assertions: When the application lock is already held, the second supervisor exits with the duplicate-instance code before constructing or starting any worker.
records.audit_note: The single-instance decision and supervisor entry are production code; the pre-held OS lock result is the deterministic boundary.

records.function: Run first-start setup and dependency checks.
records.production_entry_point: First overlay frame -> UI-host fresh-install decision -> OnboardingWizard, plus production setup-check probe
success_assertions: A fresh state opens the real wizard after the overlay startup entry, completes setup, does not reopen once marked complete, and the production runtime/dependency checks return structured results for the installed environment.
records.audit_note: The source launcher dependency installer itself remains external to this UI workflow, but both the actual fresh-start gate and reusable dependency/runtime probes execute.

records.function: Show the guided profile/setup wizard again from Settings.
records.production_entry_point: Settings Run profile setup button -> visible OnboardingWizard
success_assertions: A clean Settings dialog opens the real wizard through its visible button, while unsaved settings block the action; a completed wizard profile is persisted and visible after runtime reload.
records.audit_note: The earlier component-only audit was stale: the Settings entry button is now exercised directly.

records.function: Move backward and forward through setup steps.
records.production_entry_point: Onboarding Back and Continue buttons -> simple/advanced page sequence
success_assertions: Back returns from the name page to mode selection, Continue resumes it, advanced mode includes the provider page, and simple mode skips it without breaking later pages.
records.audit_note: Navigation is driven through real Qt buttons in both page sequences.

records.function: Choose the app display language during setup.
records.production_entry_point: Onboarding Wisp interface language combo -> live preview -> Finish setup persistence
success_assertions: Every interface-language option, including system default, survives Finish; every non-default choice invokes the live language preview route.
records.audit_note: All options exposed by LANGUAGE_OPTIONS are selected through the real combo.

records.function: Choose the assistant reply language during setup.
records.production_entry_point: Onboarding Assistant response language combo -> Finish setup -> isolated settings file
success_assertions: System default, match-user, and every named assistant-language option persist exactly.
records.audit_note: All twelve values displayed by the wizard are exercised.

records.function: Choose System, Light, or Dark appearance during setup.
records.production_entry_point: Onboarding Theme combo -> live app-theme preview -> Finish setup persistence
success_assertions: System, Light, and Dark each persist; Light and Dark invoke their normal live-preview path while System remains the already-selected default.
records.audit_note: The palette renderer is the deterministic Qt boundary; selection and persistence are production.

records.function: Choose an AI provider, model, endpoint, and API key during setup.
records.production_entry_point: Advanced setup provider/model/endpoint/key controls -> keychain boundary and named profile persistence
success_assertions: Every provider offered by the wizard accepts an editable model; every key-backed provider routes the exact key name/value to secure storage; custom persists its HTTPS endpoint; Ollama correctly stores no key.
records.audit_note: All current _PROVIDER_DEFAULTS entries run through real controls; only the OS keychain write is captured.

records.function: Sign in with a ChatGPT Plus/Pro account during setup.

success_assertions: A successful sign-in changes the visible button to Connected and selects ChatGPT when no explicit provider was chosen; an explicit provider retains precedence; skipping sign-in performs no browser launch.
records.audit_note: The browser/token service is deterministic; button, polling state, and provider precedence are real.

records.function: Add selected text to a persistent context buffer.
records.production_entry_point: Alt+Q/add_context -> native selection snapshot -> persistent drop-context list and visible badge
success_assertions: Selected text becomes one persistent Selection item and one removable context badge without entering the reply bubble.
records.audit_note: The external selection provider is the deterministic boundary.

records.function: Clear every item from the context buffer.
records.production_entry_point: clear_context -> local item list clear -> UI context-panel clear
success_assertions: Every queued item is removed locally and the visible panel is cleared even when its worker call fails.
records.audit_note: No external dependency remains.

records.function: Remove one context item without clearing the rest.
records.production_entry_point: one context badge/source X -> ui.intent.context.remove -> indexed/source-specific removal
success_assertions: Removing one attachment or one App document preserves unrelated items and only disables a group after its final source is removed.
records.audit_note: Uses production context state and UI events.

records.function: Re-enable a context source that was removed or turned off.
records.production_entry_point: removed/disabled context chip -> context choice On -> restore captured source state
success_assertions: Re-enabling App restores its removed document sources and enabling Selection attaches the available hotkey-time selection.
records.audit_note: Source capture is deterministic; state restoration and query policy are real.

records.function: Paste clipboard items into the intent overlay as context.
records.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay standard Paste -> clipboard MIME extraction -> ui.context.dropped -> intent attachment chip
success_assertions: Pasted text/files appear as removable context and a pasted bitmap is sent as vision context.
records.audit_note: The system clipboard source is the deterministic boundary.

records.function: Drop files or images onto Wisp as context.
records.production_entry_point: real Qt drag-enter/drop events on the floating icon -> MIME extraction -> badges and context_items_dropped signal
success_assertions: One actual drop gesture extracts both a text file and image, displays two badges, emits their context, and downstream context assembly preserves dropped-item priority/privacy handling.
records.audit_note: Particle/toast cosmetics are suppressed; the actual Qt drop event, extraction, panel, and signal path are real.

records.function: Preview enabled context before sending.
records.production_entry_point: begin_caller context snapshot -> ui.show_intent context_items -> visible IntentOverlay chips/previews
success_assertions: Enabled sources and per-document previews are visible before sending, while the overlay preserves state across preview refreshes.
records.audit_note: External context content is deterministic; preview generation and real Qt rendering are exercised.

records.function: Show context-source state and token estimates where available.
records.production_entry_point: context snapshot/screen metadata -> FlowController token estimates -> IntentOverlay context chips
success_assertions: Known text and screen size show estimates, deferred provider/model sources show unknown estimates, and the visible chip state remains stable after refresh.
records.audit_note: Tokenizer/provider availability is deterministic; estimation and rendering are real.

records.function: Toggle context sources with the numbered overlay keys.
records.production_entry_point: real numbered Qt key event -> IntentOverlay context state cycle -> submitted context choices -> query policy
success_assertions: A real 3 keypress turns Selection off without a refresh undoing it; enabling Selection or Clipboard at submission changes the exact context sent.
records.audit_note: Uses real Qt keyboard input plus production submission policy.

records.function: Include nearby application/window/focused-control context.
records.production_entry_point: caller App context On -> hotkey-time native snapshot -> focused app/window/control context -> query
success_assertions: Enabling App on the picker retrieves and sends the captured active app/document even when the saved default was off.
records.audit_note: OS accessibility/window APIs are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Include supported open-document content.
records.production_entry_point: App On + open docs -> captured document window -> active-document reader -> query and summary
success_assertions: Supported open-document text is fetched before the query and appears in both model context and the sent-context summary.
records.audit_note: Editor/accessibility providers are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Include current clipboard text.
records.production_entry_point: Clipboard chip On -> hotkey-time clipboard snapshot -> ambient query context
success_assertions: Enabling Clipboard sends the exact hotkey-time clipboard text even when the saved default was off.
records.audit_note: The OS clipboard is the deterministic boundary.

records.function: Include current selected text.
records.production_entry_point: caller hotkey -> native selected-text platform route -> Selection chip -> query
success_assertions: Selection is captured before the overlay takes focus on Windows, macOS, Wayland, and X11 routes and is available to the query.
records.audit_note: Accessibility/selection owners are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Include the current browser page.
records.production_entry_point: Browser On -> hotkey-time browser handle/URL -> deferred page fetch -> query context
success_assertions: The pre-overlay browser target is retained and its current page text is fetched for the query on native Windows/Linux and macOS paths.
records.audit_note: Browser automation/page content is deterministic.

records.function: Search the web when the model needs current information.
records.production_entry_point: allowed web_search model tool -> local search adapter -> source-bearing result text
success_assertions: A current-information query reaches the search adapter with its result limit and returns title, URL, and snippet.
records.audit_note: The search backend is deterministic; production tool dispatch/formatting are real.

records.function: Retrieve a specific website/page for the model.
records.production_entry_point: retrieve_website model tool -> browser fetch adapter -> page body
success_assertions: An allowed URL is fetched and returned, while the same call is blocked outside Browser/Web scope.
records.audit_note: Network/page retrieval is deterministic.

records.function: Include local Git status and diff.
records.production_entry_point: git_status/git_diff model tools -> read-only subprocess commands -> bounded output
success_assertions: Both read-only commands return successful status/diff output and contain missing Git, invalid repo, permission, timeout, and oversized-output faults.
records.audit_note: The Git executable is deterministic; command construction and output policy are real.

records.function: Fetch GitHub repository metadata.
records.production_entry_point: github_repo model tool -> OAuth token -> GitHub REST repository endpoint -> JSON result
success_assertions: Valid repository metadata returns as JSON and auth/scope/not-found/network/invalid-data failures stay controlled.
records.audit_note: OAuth and GitHub HTTP are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Fetch a GitHub issue or pull request by number.
records.production_entry_point: github_issue model tool -> validated owner/repo and number -> GitHub REST issue/PR endpoint -> JSON result
success_assertions: A positive issue number returns title/state JSON and invalid identifiers plus API failures are controlled.
records.audit_note: OAuth and GitHub HTTP are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Retrieve relevant long-term memory.
records.production_entry_point: Memory On -> frontloaded relevance retrieval, or memory_search tool -> project-aware store
success_assertions: Relevant memory is retrieved for the actual query in both frontloaded and on-demand modes while respecting project scope.
records.audit_note: Uses the production memory manager behind a deterministic store boundary.

records.function: Capture a screenshot immediately with the prompt.
records.production_entry_point: Screenshot On -> native.capture.fullscreen before send -> screenshot_b64 model input
success_assertions: Immediate mode captures the screen and sends it alongside selection text in the same query.
records.audit_note: Screen capture is deterministic.

records.function: Let the model request a screenshot only when needed.
records.production_entry_point: Screenshot Let model decide -> pre-captured native image -> capture_screen tool grant and screenshot_tool_b64
success_assertions: Model-decided mode grants capture_screen and supplies a native-captured image for the tool without attaching it as immediate prompt vision.
records.audit_note: Screen pixels and the model's decision are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Let the model request open documents only when needed.
records.production_entry_point: Open docs Let model decide -> get_context.documents grant -> open-document path reader
success_assertions: Document model mode does not frontload text; an allowed get_context call returns open-document content.
records.audit_note: Editor/path discovery is deterministic.

records.function: Let the model request browser/web context only when needed.
records.production_entry_point: Browser/Web Let model decide -> web_search/get_context.browser/retrieve_website grants -> requested adapter
success_assertions: Browser model mode offers the three scoped tools and an allowed URL context request returns current page text.
records.audit_note: Search/browser backends are deterministic.

records.function: Let the model request Git/GitHub context only when needed.
records.production_entry_point: Git/GitHub Let model decide -> git/GitHub tool grants -> successful read-only adapters
success_assertions: Model mode grants all Git/GitHub tools and each adapter returns a successful result.
records.audit_note: Git and GitHub transports are deterministic.

records.function: Let the model search memory only when needed.
records.production_entry_point: Memory Let model decide -> memory_search grant -> relevance query at tool-call time
success_assertions: Model mode excludes frontloaded memory, grants memory_search, and an allowed tool call returns relevant facts.
records.audit_note: Memory storage is deterministic.

records.function: Disable each context source for a particular shortcut or conversation.
records.production_entry_point: per-caller/conversation context Off states -> normalized policy -> capture and tool suppression
success_assertions: Every source can remain visible for estimated cost while Off prevents capture/injection/tool grants in the actual request.
records.audit_note: External providers are not called for disabled sources.

records.function: Set local file access to Off.
records.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Off -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Off persists and grants none of the five local-file tools.
records.audit_note: Uses visible Settings controls, real env persistence, config reload, and production policy.

records.function: Set local file access to Read only.
records.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Read only -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Read only persists and grants list/read while withholding create/edit/write.
records.audit_note: Uses visible Settings controls, real env persistence, config reload, and production policy.

records.function: Set local file access to Ask before writing.
records.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Ask before writing -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Ask persists, grants all five tools, and writes require an approval decision with a diff.
records.audit_note: Uses visible Settings, real persistence/policy, and the real approval/toolbox path.

records.function: Set local file access to Write automatically.
records.production_entry_point: Intent shortcut File access combo Write automatically -> Save changes -> config reload -> runtime tool policy
success_assertions: Write automatically persists, grants all five tools, and each tool completes its disk operation.
records.audit_note: Uses visible Settings, real persistence/policy, and real isolated filesystem operations.

records.function: Limit file access to configured root folders.
records.production_entry_point: all live file tools -> configured root resolution -> containment/symlink checks -> operation
success_assertions: All five tools work inside a configured root and all reject parent and symlink escapes.
records.audit_note: Uses real isolated filesystem operations.

records.function: Block private files with configurable glob patterns.
records.production_entry_point: live file path -> configured blocked-glob matcher -> in-band refusal
success_assertions: Blocked private paths are refused by the live tool and changed blocked-glob settings take effect after real brain-worker reload.
records.audit_note: Uses real path policy with filesystem/worker boundaries.

records.function: Limit browser, ambient-document, and tool-document context sizes.
records.production_entry_point: configured browser/ambient-document/tool-document limits -> each production context reader
success_assertions: The browser extractor receives the browser limit, the ambient window reader receives the ambient-document limit, and the model document tool receives the tool-document limit.
records.audit_note: The test executes all three production dispatch paths; HTTP, accessibility, and file extraction are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Open the full-screen snip overlay.
records.production_entry_point: Full-screen mode click on the visible SnipOverlay
success_assertions: The production overlay emits the complete virtual-desktop region and no cancellation.
records.audit_note: Uses a real Qt mouse event.

records.function: Draw a rectangular screen region and attach it.
records.production_entry_point: Snip Area mode -> real mouse drag -> normalized/scaled capture region signal
success_assertions: A rectangular drag emits the scaled usable region and closes the overlay.
records.audit_note: Uses the real Qt snip overlay.

records.function: Capture the full screen.
records.production_entry_point: Snip Full toolbar mode -> virtual desktop geometry -> full-region signal
success_assertions: Full mode emits the complete virtual desktop from either toolbar selection or canvas click.
records.audit_note: Monitor geometry is deterministic; the Qt controls are real.

records.function: Capture the current application/window bounds.
records.production_entry_point: valid current-app region -> Snip App toolbar mode -> copied app-region signal
success_assertions: App mode is available only for valid bounds and emits an independent copy of those bounds.
records.audit_note: Window bounds are deterministic; toolbar behavior is real.

records.function: Switch between Area, App, and Full capture modes.
records.production_entry_point: visible Area/App/Full toolbar buttons -> mode state -> mode-specific capture behavior
success_assertions: Each visible capture-mode control selects only its documented region behavior.
records.audit_note: Uses the real Qt overlay and toolbar.

records.function: Cancel capture with Escape.
records.production_entry_point: Escape key on the visible SnipOverlay
success_assertions: The focused capture overlay emits cancellation once.
records.audit_note: Uses a real Qt key event after the deferred focus sequence.

records.function: Attach the resulting image to the intent picker.
records.production_entry_point: Intent Screenshot chip -> visible snip overlay result -> native region capture -> restored picker attachment
success_assertions: The selected region is captured once, the active prompt/chips are restored, and the resulting image is retained for submission.
records.audit_note: The monitor pixels are deterministic; intent/snip/native routing is production code.

records.function: Ask a vision-capable model about the captured image.
records.production_entry_point: picker image or snip capture -> screenshot_b64 -> brain.query -> vision response
success_assertions: Clipboard and snip images both reach the vision request, which preserves the user's prompt plus ambient and memory context and returns an answer.
records.audit_note: Image bytes and provider response are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Route images through the configured Image model and its fallbacks.
records.production_entry_point: image-bearing request -> configured Image primary route -> ordered Image fallbacks -> answer
success_assertions: The Image primary, first empty fallback, and second successful fallback are attempted in order with the exact same prompt and image payload.
records.audit_note: Provider calls are deterministic; route selection and fallback behavior are production code.

records.function: Show an image returned by a model in the reply bubble or chat.
records.production_entry_point: model generated-image event -> reply bubble/chat image renderer -> bounded thumbnail
success_assertions: An assistant image with or without text appears as a valid bounded thumbnail in both reply bubble and chat.
records.audit_note: Uses real Qt image decoding/layout against an isolated PNG.

records.function: Show listening, transcript, progress, answer, warning, and error text beside the icon.
records.production_entry_point: runtime UI text events -> SpeechBubble listening/transcript/progress/answer/warning/error methods
success_assertions: Every documented runtime text state becomes visible and later states correctly replace transient content.
records.audit_note: Uses the real Qt bubble and the public methods invoked by UI-host dispatch.

records.function: Stream answer text into the bubble.
records.production_entry_point: brain.query reply.chunk stream -> FlowController -> ui.reply.chunk -> SpeechBubble document
success_assertions: Incremental model chunks retain their exact joined answer order in the bubble and persist as the same chat answer.
records.audit_note: The provider stream is deterministic; production stream routing/rendering are real.

records.function: Reveal words progressively.
records.production_entry_point: reply chunks -> SpeechBubble WPM/timestamp reveal units -> timed visible document
success_assertions: Latin text reveals by timed units, CJK reveals character by character, and completion resets timers without stranding text.
records.audit_note: Uses the real Qt reveal timers with deterministic ticks.

records.function: Highlight the currently spoken word when timestamps are available.
records.production_entry_point: audio word timestamps -> SpeechBubble timestamp scheduler -> active word formatting and viewport follow
success_assertions: Timestamped Latin/CJK units advance the highlighted word through the final visible unit while the viewport follows it.
records.audit_note: Audio timestamps are deterministic; scheduler and Qt document formatting are real.

records.function: Use normal timed reveal when the TTS provider has no word timestamps.
records.production_entry_point: TTS result without word timestamps -> WPM fallback reveal -> drained reply completion
success_assertions: Without timestamps, reply.done does not flush all text at once; the normal reveal drains and completes safely even with invalid WPM configuration.
records.audit_note: TTS capability is deterministic; fallback dispatch/reveal are production code.

records.function: Hold the fast-forward control to speed text and speech.
records.production_entry_point: visible fast-forward mouse press/release -> ui.bubble.speed -> audio.speed_boost and bubble reveal speed
success_assertions: Holding the visible control enables both bubble/audio speed and releasing it disables both exactly once.
records.audit_note: Uses real Qt mouse events plus production flow dispatch; audio output is deterministic.

records.function: Stop/cancel the current reply from the close/stop control.
records.production_entry_point: visible reply close/stop click -> ui.bubble.stop -> brain.cancel/audio.stop -> idle
success_assertions: The real stop control clears the bubble, cancels the exact active model request and speech, returns idle, and rejects late output.
records.audit_note: Uses a real Qt click and an overlapped live stream/cancel race.

records.function: Dismiss a non-cancellable informational notice.
records.production_entry_point: informational notice close control -> dismiss-only bubble reset
success_assertions: Closing a notice hides it without emitting backend stop, while the same control remains cancellable for active reading/replies.
records.audit_note: Runs the production close hit path with deterministic pointer events.

records.function: Click the bubble to open the full chat.
records.production_entry_point: real text-area click on completed reply bubble -> click callback -> full chat
success_assertions: A completed reply click opens chat once; listening/thinking clicks do not and the click cannot trigger speed boost.
records.audit_note: Uses real Qt mouse clicks on the visible text and shell surfaces.

records.function: Drag the bubble/icon group.
records.production_entry_point: bubble shell pointer press/move/release -> top-level move -> companion icon callback
success_assertions: A pointer drag moves the bubble/icon group and is not misread as a chat-opening click.
records.audit_note: Uses real Qt pointer events; compositor system-move is the platform boundary.

records.function: Pause auto-hide while the user hovers or interacts.
records.production_entry_point: bubble hover/text interaction -> auto-hide hold/latch -> resumed or cancelled hide timer
success_assertions: Hover pauses then resumes auto-hide, while wheel/click interaction latches the current bubble open.
records.audit_note: Runs the real bubble interaction and hide-timer policy.

records.function: Wheel-scroll long bubble text.
records.production_entry_point: wheel event over bubble document -> SpeechBubble manual viewport -> immediate repaint
success_assertions: Wheel input moves the visible long-text window immediately without replacing the complete selectable document.
records.audit_note: Uses the production Qt wheel path with deterministic deltas.

records.function: Snap manual scrolling back to the spoken/highlighted line.
records.production_entry_point: manual bubble scroll + active speech highlight -> snap timer -> highlighted viewport
success_assertions: While speech is active, snapping clears manual offset and returns the viewport to the currently highlighted line.
records.audit_note: Uses production scroll/highlight state and viewport application.

records.function: Select text inside the bubble.
records.production_entry_point: real selectable QTextBrowser inside bubble -> text selection -> preserved selection across updates
success_assertions: The full bubble text is mouse/keyboard selectable, avoids controls, and preserves the selected range while highlighting updates.
records.audit_note: Uses the real Qt document and selection cursor.

records.function: Copy selected bubble text.
records.production_entry_point: selected bubble range -> real context menu Copy selected text -> system clipboard
success_assertions: Copy selected text writes exactly the selected range and is absent when there is no selection.
records.audit_note: Uses the production Qt context menu/actions; clipboard storage is real Qt state.

records.function: Copy the full bubble text.
records.production_entry_point: bubble context menu Copy full bubble text -> system clipboard
success_assertions: Copy full bubble text writes the complete reply whether or not a smaller range is selected.
records.audit_note: Uses the production Qt context menu/actions; clipboard storage is real Qt state.

records.function: Display assistant-created images.
records.production_entry_point: image-only assistant result -> FlowController persistence -> bubble/chat thumbnail renderer
success_assertions: An assistant-created image is retained as an assistant turn and remains visible in both compact and full-chat surfaces without fallback text.
records.audit_note: Generated bytes are deterministic; production persistence and Qt rendering are real.

records.function: Edit or delete UI Lab labels from selected bubble text when that add-on is available.
records.production_entry_point: selected bubble text -> add-on context actions -> UI Lab edit/delete callbacks
success_assertions: With selected text, UI Lab edit/delete actions appear and receive the exact range; without a selection they are absent, and saved labels render on bubble/chat surfaces.
records.audit_note: The menu and add-on manager are production code; modal edit response is outside this surface test.

records.function: Open persistent multi-turn chat.
records.production_entry_point: visible ChatWindow -> persisted conversation history -> multi-turn send stream
success_assertions: The real chat window opens, displays stored history, sends a turn, persists the answer, and continues an older conversation.
records.audit_note: Qt controls and conversation logic are real; the model stream is deterministic.

records.function: Start a new chat from the button or `Ctrl+N`.
records.production_entry_point: New chat button and Ctrl+N shortcut -> ChatWindow.start_new_conversation
success_assertions: Both visible button and keyboard shortcut create and select a new conversation under the chosen project.
records.audit_note: Both user entry routes use real Qt events.

records.function: Send with Enter and insert a newline with Shift+Enter.
records.production_entry_point: chat composer Return/Shift+Return events -> send/newline handlers
success_assertions: Shift+Enter inserts a newline without sending; Enter sends the exact multiline text once.
records.audit_note: Uses the focused production QTextEdit and its event filter.

records.function: Stream assistant text, reasoning summaries, tool activity, and images.
records.production_entry_point: brain chat events -> FlowController -> UI-host chat stream -> ChatWindow renderer/persistence
success_assertions: Text, thought/progress/tool segments, metadata, and image-only results retain order, render, and persist without becoming the wrong final answer.
records.audit_note: Worker/provider emissions are deterministic boundaries; runtime routing and Qt rendering are production.

records.function: Continue a prior conversation.
records.production_entry_point: history row click -> active conversation -> Enter send with retained turns
success_assertions: Selecting an older chat and sending includes its prior user turn and appends the follow-up to that conversation only.
records.audit_note: History selection and send are real controls.

records.function: Choose whether an overlay request starts a new chat or continues an existing chat.
records.production_entry_point: IntentOverlay project/mode/history hit regions and popup actions -> conversation_choice
success_assertions: Real overlay clicks switch new/continue mode, filter history by project, and select the exact conversation index.
records.audit_note: The overlay paint hit regions and QMenu actions are production; popup display is captured offscreen.

records.function: Switch conversations from history.
records.production_entry_point: ChatWindow history row button -> active stack page/conversation
success_assertions: Clicking each tested history row selects the matching conversation and subsequent sends stay there.
records.audit_note: Uses real sidebar buttons.

records.function: Group conversation history by project.
records.production_entry_point: conversation project_id/pinned state -> grouped ChatWindow sidebar
success_assertions: General and named-project conversations appear under the correct groups while their real rows remain actionable.
records.audit_note: The grouping algorithm and visible labels are production.

records.function: Create a project.
records.production_entry_point: project combo New project option -> name dialog -> project callback/list refresh
success_assertions: Selecting New project creates it, selects it, exposes it in project actions, and the store round-trips it.
records.audit_note: Only the modal text response is supplied deterministically.

records.function: Choose the project for new chats.
records.production_entry_point: project combo selection -> new-chat project_id
success_assertions: General, an existing named project, and a newly created project assign the expected project_id to new chats.
records.audit_note: Selection and new-chat creation use production controls.

records.function: Scope memory to the selected project.
records.production_entry_point: selected project -> active memory scope -> save/retrieve filtering and UI-host memory proxy
success_assertions: Project facts are saved under the active project, excluded from another project, and UI add/update calls retain their explicit project scope.
records.audit_note: Memory storage is real but isolated; semantic model retrieval is deterministic.

records.function: Add a conversation to a project.
records.production_entry_point: conversation options Add to project submenu -> conversation project_id/persist/sidebar refresh
success_assertions: The real submenu moves the selected conversation to the chosen project and persists the mutation.
records.audit_note: Menu construction and action routing are production.

records.function: Pin or unpin a conversation.
records.production_entry_point: conversation options Pin/Unpin -> persisted pinned ordering state
success_assertions: Pin and Unpin appear according to current state, toggle it through the real menu, and round-trip in storage.
records.audit_note: Uses actual sidebar menu buttons and actions.

records.function: Rename a conversation.
records.production_entry_point: conversation options Rename -> dialog -> title override/persist/sidebar refresh
success_assertions: A confirmed name becomes the visible tooltip and persists; cancellation/empty/error cases do not corrupt the old title.
records.audit_note: Only modal responses and persistence failures are deterministic boundaries.

records.function: Delete a conversation after confirmation.
records.production_entry_point: conversation options Delete -> confirmation -> history removal/persist
success_assertions: Confirmed deletion removes only the target; rejected or failed deletion preserves history.
records.audit_note: The button/menu/action path is real; confirmation answers and injected save failures are deterministic.

records.function: Browse files associated with a conversation.
records.production_entry_point: conversation options Browse conversation files -> persist -> shared reveal helper
success_assertions: The real action persists first and reveals the exact conversation record.
records.audit_note: OS file-manager launch is the captured boundary.

records.function: Show conversation and message timestamps.
records.production_entry_point: conversation/message metadata -> sidebar and bubble timestamp labels
success_assertions: Stored ISO metadata is formatted and displayed on both conversation rows and message headers.
records.audit_note: Formatting and Qt label rendering are production.

records.function: Attach one or more files or images with the file picker.
records.production_entry_point: attachment button -> QFileDialog selection -> shared MIME attachment pipeline -> send
success_assertions: Clicking the attachment button queues the selected file, shows its name, and supplies its content/reference to the sent turn.
records.audit_note: The modal returns an isolated path; button and extraction/send paths are production.

records.function: Drag and drop files/images into chat.
records.production_entry_point: Qt drag-enter/drop events -> shared MIME attachment pipeline -> send
success_assertions: A real image MIME drag is accepted, queued, saved as an attachment, sent, and rendered.
records.audit_note: Uses QApplication event delivery to the production drop handlers.

records.function: Show pending attachment names and context.
records.production_entry_point: picker/drop pending attachment state -> visible chip/context -> user turn/model payload
success_assertions: Pending names are visible before send, context reaches the request, and references survive reopening.
records.audit_note: Files and storage are isolated; UI and model-payload assembly are production.

records.function: Display attached and returned image thumbnails.
records.production_entry_point: user/assistant image attachments -> ChatWindow thumbnail renderer
success_assertions: Both a dropped user image and an image-only assistant result create non-null visible thumbnails.
records.audit_note: Image bytes are deterministic; Qt decoding/scaling/rendering are real.

records.function: Configure App context per conversation.
records.production_entry_point: App context chip -> state menu -> per-conversation policy -> send/runtime policy
success_assertions: Off, On, and Let model decide persist per chat and reach the request policy without stale overrides.
records.audit_note: All exposed menu states run through real actions.

records.function: Configure Browser/Web context per conversation.
records.production_entry_point: Browser/Web context chip -> state menu -> per-conversation policy -> runtime fetch
success_assertions: All three menu states persist per chat; On fetches browser context and Off suppresses it.
records.audit_note: Browser capture is deterministic; policy/menu/runtime route are production.

records.function: Configure Selection context per conversation.
records.production_entry_point: Selection context chip -> Off/On menu -> capture -> per-conversation request
success_assertions: Off and On are menu-driven; On requests interactive capture and the captured selection reaches the chosen chat only.
records.audit_note: Native selection result is deterministic.

records.function: Configure Clipboard context per conversation.
records.production_entry_point: Clipboard context chip -> Off/On menu -> per-conversation request policy
success_assertions: Both menu states persist per conversation and the final absolute policy reaches runtime.
records.audit_note: Clipboard contents are an external deterministic boundary.

records.function: Configure Screenshot context per conversation.
records.production_entry_point: Screenshot context chip -> state menu -> interactive snip/auto policy -> request
success_assertions: Off, On, and model-decide states work; On requests capture and attaches its image without unrelated file permission.
records.audit_note: Capture pixels are deterministic; chip/runtime paths are production.

records.function: Configure Git/GitHub context per conversation.
records.production_entry_point: Git/GitHub context chip -> state menu -> per-conversation tool grants
success_assertions: All three states persist per chat and reach the absolute runtime policy/tool grant calculation.

records.function: Capture screen/app regions on multi-monitor desktops.
records.production_entry_point: Visible Snip overlay area/app/full controls -> emitted capture region with live virtual-desktop origin and monitor DPR
success_assertions: Area drag, app target, and full virtual desktop produce usable regions; live negative/positive monitor origins and 1x/2x DPR changes are read at selection time, while macOS remains in logical points.
records.audit_note: The real Qt overlay and visible capture-mode controls run offscreen; only the physical monitor topology is modeled.

records.function: Use macOS helper/Accessibility flows where native permissions are required.
records.production_entry_point: macOS native worker route -> Carbon helper / AX selection / AX paste or activation-plus-Cmd+V
success_assertions: The helper process reports ready, forwards an event, and terminates; AX selection and in-place paste work, fallback activation plus Cmd+V works, and capture coordinates stay in macOS logical points.
records.audit_note: Carbon, ApplicationServices, Quartz, and AppKit are external OS boundaries; every Wisp helper/protocol and fallback decision is real.

records.function: Use Linux AT-SPI/X11/Wayland-compatible fallbacks where available.
records.production_entry_point: Linux native worker route -> pynput hotkeys / Wayland AT-SPI selection / X11 PRIMARY selection
success_assertions: Linux selects and starts its pynput backend, emits the registered event, reads focused text through both Wayland AT-SPI and X11 PRIMARY, and pastes to the captured external target.
records.audit_note: The display server and input hook are deterministic boundaries; Linux session routing and Wisp event/paste behavior are real.

records.function: Use Windows native focused-control, clipboard, and hotkey integrations.
records.production_entry_point: Windows native worker route -> RegisterHotKey / UIA selected range / clipboard and paste
success_assertions: Windows selects its RegisterHotKey backend, emits the registered action, reads UIA selection, reselects the captured UIA range before paste, obeys both clipboard policies, and translates live monitor origin/DPR changes.
records.audit_note: Win32/UIAutomation/clipboard calls are external boundaries; Wisp's anchored-control and multi-monitor logic remains real.

records.function: Keep windows on screen and provide standard close/minimize controls where appropriate.
records.production_entry_point: Real top-level Qt surfaces -> shared window chrome and fit_window_to_screen -> title-bar controls
success_assertions: An oversized off-screen popup is fitted within the current screen, survives replacement after destruction, contains window-manager refusal, and exposes working minimize, maximize, and close controls.
records.audit_note: Real Qt top-level windows are shown offscreen; native window-manager move/pinning refusal is the captured desktop boundary.

records.function: Use the operating-system file browser to reveal logs, reports, task folders, add-ons, and conversation files.
records.production_entry_point: Visible log/report/task/add-on controls and conversation menu action -> shared OS reveal boundary
success_assertions: The actual Open-log, crash-report, task result/scope, Open add-ons, and Browse conversation actions route their exact existing targets; every file/folder x Windows/macOS/Linux case produces the correct native command.
records.audit_note: Qt controls and target selection are real; process creation for Explorer, Finder, or xdg-open is captured to keep CI deterministic.
